data.title: Lennar: Hold Rating Reaffirmed as Operational Gains Offset by Cautious Outlook; $100 Price Target Maintained
data.description:
Lennar, the Consumer Cyclical sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst yesterday. Analyst Susan Maklari from Goldman Sachs maintained a Hold rating on the stock and has a $100.00 price target.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Susan Maklari has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors reflecting both operational progress and lingering headwinds. While Lennar delivered an earnings beat versus expectations, much of the upside came from lower overhead, non-core segments, and other below-the-line items rather than a meaningful outperformance in its core homebuilding business.

Homebuilding revenue and margins were broadly consistent with forecasts, and guidance for near-term deliveries and full-year closings was trimmed, signaling a more cautious outlook amid higher mortgage rates and macro uncertainty. At the same time, Lennar is showing tangible improvements in inventory management, build cycle times, and gross margins, which support the current valuation but do not yet justify a more favorable rating. The price target is unchanged at $100, indicating limited upside from current levels and reinforcing a balanced risk-reward profile.

In another report released today, Wells Fargo also assigned a Hold rating to the stock with a $90.00 price target.

LEN’s price has also changed moderately for the past six months – from $119.150 to $94.950, which is a -20.31% drop .
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Vistra Maintained at Buy as Analyst Trims Price Target to $209 While Highlighting AI-Driven Growth Opportunities
data.description:
Analyst Carly Davenport from Goldman Sachs maintained a Buy rating on Vistra Corp and decreased the price target to $209.00 from $212.00.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Carly Davenport has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Vistra’s strategic positioning and new growth avenues. The company’s role as a founding investor and preferred power provider in the newly formed Helix Digital Infrastructure platform gives it direct exposure to rising power needs from hyperscale AI data centers, supported by over $10 billion in committed capital.

Davenport views this structure as an attractive, incremental way for Vistra to capture hyperscaler demand beyond traditional bilateral power contracts. While the official price target was modestly reduced from $212 to $209, she still sees meaningful upside for the stock based on Vistra’s ability to monetize AI-driven load growth and leverage multiple channels for long-term value creation.

Davenport covers the Utilities sector, focusing on stocks such as Duke Energy, Eversource Energy, and Ameren. According to TipRanks, Davenport has an average return of 2.6% and a 53.66% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released on June 4, Wells Fargo also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $259.00 price target.
